card.front: At which three types of plate margin do physical processes lead to earthquakes and volcanic activity in this specification?
card.back: Constructive, destructive and conservative plate margins.
card.explanation: Physical processes at all three named margin types are linked to earthquakes and volcanic activity.
card.memoryTip: Use the initials C-D-C: constructive, destructive, conservative.

choices.text: Constructive, destructive and conservative plate margins.
choices.feedback: Correct: these are the three specified plate-margin types.
These belong to the general atmospheric circulation model.
These classify hazard effects, not plate margins.
These are tectonic risk-reduction approaches, not margin types.

card.sourceExcerpt:
Physical processes taking place at different
types of plate margin (constructive, destructive
and conservative) that lead to earthquakes and
volcanic activity.

issues: The stem and explanation imply that all three margin types lead to both earthquakes and volcanic activity. Conservative margins are associated with earthquakes but not volcanic activity; the excerpt names the margins collectively and does not support this per-margin claim.
The volcano cue reinforces the misleading association for conservative margins.
learnerValue: The C-D-C mnemonic usefully retrieves the three named margin types.
